L1.area: Storage backend data transfer and import processing

L2.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the storage backup import pipeline where a tar archive iteration loop only checks for end-of-archive errors. When a truncated or corrupted archive produces a non-EOF read error, the tar header variable is nil but is immediately dereferenced to access its name field, causing a daemon panic and denial of service.

L3.area: S3 storage bucket transfer and file upload logic
L3.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the S3 bucket backup restore pipeline where a tar archive iteration loop only checks for end-of-archive (EOF) errors. When a truncated or corrupted backup archive produces a non-EOF read error during bucket import, the tar header variable is nil but is immediately dereferenced to access its name field, causing a daemon panic and denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: TransferManager.UploadAllFiles only checks for io.EOF from tr.Next(), so a non-EOF error (e.g. from a truncated archive) leaves hdr as nil, and the subsequent hdr.Name access causes a nil pointer dereference panic.
